chunks.text: Costas J. Spanos is Professor Emeritus in EECS at UC Berkeley. He received his Electrical Engineering Diploma from the National Technical University of Athens, Greece in 1980 and his M.S. and Ph.D. degrees in Electrical and Computer Engineering from Carnegie Mellon University in 1981 and 1985, respectively. From 1985 to 1988, he worked at Digital Equipment Corporation's advanced Computer-Aided Design group on statistical characterization, simulation and diagnosis of VLSI processes.
chunks.question: What degrees did Costas Spanos earn and from where?
chunks.answer: Diploma from NTU Athens, M.S./Ph.D. from Carnegie Mellon.

chunks.text: In 1988, Spanos joined UC Berkeley's EECS faculty. He served as Director of the Berkeley Microfabrication Laboratory (1994-2000), Director of the Electronics Research Laboratory (2004-2005), Associate Dean for Research in the College of Engineering (2004-2008), Associate Chair for EECS (2008-2010), and Chair of the EECS Department (2010-2012). He was editor of IEEE Transactions on Semiconductor Manufacturing from 1991 to 1994.
chunks.question: What leadership roles did Spanos hold at UC Berkeley?
chunks.answer: Lab director, associate dean, department chair roles.

chunks.text: Spanos has published over 200 refereed articles, received best paper awards, and co-authored a textbook on semiconductor manufacturing. His research interests include statistical analysis in IC design/fabrication, and novel sensors/computer-aided techniques in semiconductor manufacturing. He was elected IEEE Fellow in 2000 for contributions to semiconductor manufacturing.
chunks.question: What are Spanos's main research contributions?
chunks.answer: Statistical analysis and sensors in semiconductor manufacturing.

chunks.text: His research areas include Power and Energy (ENE), Integrated Circuits (INC), and Physical Electronics (PHY) with focus on solid-state devices and semiconductor manufacturing. He is associated with research centers: Berkeley Power and Energy Center (BPEC), CITRIS People and Robots (CPAR), and Integrated Modeling Process and Computation for Technology (IMPACT).
chunks.question: Which research centers is Spanos associated with?

chunks.text: Selected publications include the textbook 'Fundamentals of Semiconductor Manufacturing and Process Control' (2006), patents on process control methods (2006, 2004), and papers on wafer sensors, spatial uniformity, and scatterometry in IEEE Transactions on Semiconductor Manufacturing (2005, 2001).
chunks.question: What textbook did Spanos co-author?
chunks.answer: Fundamentals of Semiconductor Manufacturing and Process Control.

chunks.text: Selected honors include the Building and Environment Best Paper Award (2018), Distinguished Berkeley Faculty Mentor Award (2016), and IEEE Fellow (2000). His contact information includes office at 510 Cory Hall, phone 510-643-6776, fax 642-2739, and administrative contact Eva Leach (eva.leach@berkeley.edu).
chunks.question: What awards did Spanos receive?
chunks.answer: Best paper, faculty mentor, and IEEE Fellow awards.
